weekRowOffsets.semantics: Start row for each observed week, followed by terminal rowCount.

dataQuality.dateBasis: Dates and generatedAtUtc use the maximum aggregate-safe cleaned event date, never the current clock.

precinctsByBorough.knownPrecinctPolicy: Each known precinct is assigned to the borough with its largest all-time weekly aggregate count; ties use borough lexical order.
precinctsByBorough.unknownPrecinctPolicy: UNKNOWN is listed only for boroughs with observed UNKNOWN-precinct counts.

limitations: Counts describe reported aggregate complaint events and do not explain causality.
The latest source week can be partial and is excluded from default comparisons.
Reporting delays, classification changes, and later revisions can change counts.
Anomalies are observed deviations, hotspots are aggregate concentration signals, and forecasts are model estimates; these are distinct measures.
Outputs are decision-support context and are not grounds for person-level or enforcement action.
Forecast outputs do not include uncertainty intervals and may follow a partial source week.

observed.comparisonSemantics: Compare the most recent four complete selected weeks with the prior four complete selected weeks, or equal shorter windows when fewer than eight complete selected weeks are available; never include later observations.
observed.dateFilterSemantics: Inclusive week_start range over Monday-based weekly aggregate buckets.
observed.latestWeekNote: The latest week is retained for freshness but excluded from the default range and comparisons when incomplete.

historicalError.filterSemantics: Historical errors are not recomputed for active dashboard filters.
historicalError.scope: overall model backtest across all segments

limitations: No prediction interval or other uncertainty interval is available; only a point estimate is supplied.
The forecast horizon follows a source extract whose latest observed week may be partial.
